Cassia fenestralis, also known as "Golden Shower Tree," is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cassia fenestralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cassia fenestralis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be found in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures.
